title: WOS Studio (Authoring) — Workflow Intent
description: Studio WorkflowIntent per studio-authoring/specs/workflow-intent.md. The user-facing draft that compiles to a `$wosWorkflow` artifact (per compiler-contract.md). Carries 16 user-facing element kinds (phase / step / decision / review / notice / deadline / appeal / exception / hold / data-collection / evidence-request / system-check / AI-assistance / manual-override / completion-outcome / phase-end), each with a bridge field that names the kernel construct produced. Bridge.kernelKind is INFERRED for 14 unambiguous kinds (per workflow-intent.md 'Bridge inference rules', SA-MUST-wfi-005); REQUIRED only on the 2 ambiguous kinds (`step`, `system-check`). The 6 bridge construct kinds (WorkflowStepMapping, LifecycleTagMapping, TransitionMapping, TimerMapping, TaskMapping, CaseFileMapping) are co-located here per CM §6.1 corrected pattern (2026-05-01) — they're products of bridge-inference compilation, not author-written PolicyObjects.
$comment: Promotion status (Stage-3, 2026-05-01): MVP shape; element body shapes are illustrative-and-flexible. Per-kind body validation lives in Stage-4 lint rules.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker for the Studio WorkflowIntent

$wosStudioWorkflowIntent.description: Document marker pinning this artifact to the Studio WorkflowIntent 1.0 schema.

impactLevel.description: Impact classification per parent kernel/spec.md §6. Drives WOS embedded-block conditionality at compile time per substrate-choice (CM §5).

x-lm.intent: Risk-profile classifier — drives substrate-side embedded-block conditional emission per CM §5

wosVersionPin.description: WOS version claim string per parent RELEASE-STREAMS.md. Required at lifecycle 'mapped' or later.
examples: kernel@1.0, governance@1.0, ai@0.5, signature@1.0, custody@1.0, advanced@0.3, assurance@1.0

effectivenessRef.description: Workflow-level Effectiveness ref (jurisdictional + temporal scope). Element-level effectivenessRef on individual WorkflowElements narrows further.

policyObjectRefs.description: PolicyObjects this intent is derived from.

elements.description: WorkflowElements (16 user-facing kinds). Each carries `kind` + body + bridge.

expertModeView.description: Mapping from intent elements to compiled $wosWorkflow JSON paths.

kind.description: User-facing element kind. The bridge.kernelKind is INFERRED from this for 14 of 16 kinds; REQUIRED on `step` + `system-check` (per workflow-intent.md 'Bridge inference rules').

x-lm.intent: Element kind drives bridge inference (kernelKind) and body shape — load-bearing for compilation routing

body.description: Kind-specific body. Stage-3 accepts flexible body shape; Stage-4 lint rules enforce per-kind required fields.

position.description: Structural placement (phase id, sequence index, parent step ref).

BridgeAssertion.description: Bridge from element to kernel construct. `kernelKind` is OPTIONAL — inferred from element.kind for unambiguous cases per workflow-intent.md 'Bridge inference rules' (SA-MUST-wfi-005). REQUIRED on ambiguous kinds (`step`, `system-check`). Body fields name the specific kernel construct: `stateName` (compound/atomic/terminal state), `taskRef`, `transitionId`, `timerRef`, `noticeId`, `subFlowEntry`, `agentRef`, `guardRef`. The 6 bridge construct kinds (WorkflowStepMapping, LifecycleTagMapping, TransitionMapping, TimerMapping, TaskMapping, CaseFileMapping) are products of bridge-inference and live as projection records, not as separate authoring objects.

kernelKind.description: Kernel construct discriminant. OPTIONAL when element.kind is unambiguous (inferred per workflow-intent.md 'Bridge inference rules'); REQUIRED on `step` (atomic-state | task) and `system-check` (task | transition-guard).

guardKind.description: Guard form (FEL string vs decisionTable per parent Kernel §4.5.1).
